streams.description: Focuses on technology, engineering, data science, and non-medical scientific fields. Ideal for students with strong analytical and problem-solving skills and an interest in mathematics and logic.

core_subjects.subject: Physics
topics: Physical World and Measurement
Mechanics (Kinematics, Laws of Motion, Work, Energy, Power, System of Particles, Rotational Motion, Gravitation)
Properties of Bulk Matter (Elasticity, Fluid Mechanics, Surface Tension, Viscosity, Thermal Properties of Matter)
Thermodynamics (Heat, Temperature, Laws of Thermodynamics, Kinetic Theory of Gases)
Oscillations and Waves (Simple Harmonic Motion, Doppler Effect, Wave Optics)
Electrostatics (Electric Charges, Fields, Potential, Capacitance)
Current Electricity (Ohm's Law, Circuits, Kirchhoff's Laws, Electrical Instruments)
Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism (Magnetic Fields, Forces, Torque, Earth's Magnetism)
Electromagnetic Induction and AC (Faraday's Law, Lenz's Law, AC Circuits, Transformers)
Electromagnetic Waves
Optics (Ray Optics, Reflection, Refraction, Lenses, Prisms, Microscopes, Telescopes)
Modern Physics (Dual Nature of Matter, Photoelectric Effect, Atoms, Nuclei, Radioactivity)
Electronic Devices (Semiconductors, Diodes, Transistors, Logic Gates)
Communication Systems

core_subjects.subject: Chemistry
topics: Basic Concepts (Mole Concept, Stoichiometry, Atomic Structure, Periodicity)
Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure (VSEPR, Hybridization, VBT, MOT)
States of Matter (Gases, Liquids, Solids)
Thermodynamics and Equilibrium (Enthalpy, Entropy, Gibbs Free Energy, Chemical Equilibrium, Ionic Equilibrium)
Redox Reactions and Electrochemistry (Electrochemical Cells, Nernst Equation, Electrolysis)
Chemical Kinetics (Rate of Reaction, Order of Reaction, Arrhenius Equation)
Solutions (Colligative Properties, Vapour Pressure)
Surface Chemistry (Adsorption, Colloids, Emulsions)
Metallurgy (Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements)
s-Block, p-Block, d-Block, and f-Block Elements (Hydrogen, Alkali Metals, Alkaline Earth Metals, Boron, Carbon, Nitrogen, Oxygen, Halogen, Noble Gases, Transition Metals)
Coordination Compounds (Nomenclature, Isomerism, VBT, CFT)
Organic Chemistry - Basic Principles (GOC, Nomenclature, Isomerism, Reaction Mechanisms)
Hydrocarbons (Alkanes, Alkenes, Alkynes, Arenes, Aromaticity)
Organic Compounds Containing Halogens (Haloalkanes, Haloarenes)
Organic Compounds Containing Oxygen (Alcohols, Phenols, Ethers, Aldehydes, Ketones, Carboxylic Acids)
Organic Compounds Containing Nitrogen (Amines, Cyanides, Isocyanides, Diazonium Salts)
Biomolecules (Carbohydrates, Proteins, Vitamins, Nucleic Acids, Enzymes)
Polymers (Classification, Polymerization, Common Polymers)
Chemistry in Everyday Life (Drugs, Soaps, Detergents, Food Preservatives)

core_subjects.subject: Mathematics
topics: Sets and Functions (Sets, Relations, Functions, Trigonometric Functions, Inverse Trigonometric Functions)
Algebra (Principle of Mathematical Induction, Complex Numbers, Quadratic Equations, Linear Inequalities, Permutations, Combinations, Binomial Theorem, Sequences and Series, Matrices, Determinants)
Coordinate Geometry (Straight Lines, Conic Sections - Circle, Parabola, Ellipse, Hyperbola, 3D Geometry)
Calculus (Limits, Derivatives, Continuity and Differentiability, Application of Derivatives, Integrals, Application of Integrals, Differential Equations)
Vectors and 3D Geometry (Vectors, Scalar and Vector Products, Lines, Planes)
Linear Programming (LPP)
Mathematical Reasoning
Statistics and Probability (Mean, Median, Mode, Variance, Standard Deviation, Correlation, Regression, Probability, Conditional Probability, Bayes' Theorem, Probability Distributions)

core_subjects.subject: English
topics: Reading Comprehension (Unseen Passages, Note Making, Summarizing)
Writing Skills (Notice, Letter Writing - Formal/Informal, Report Writing, Article, Speech, Debate, Resume, Job Application)
Grammar (Tenses, Modals, Voice, Narration, Clauses, Transformation of Sentences)
Literature (Prose, Poetry, Supplementary Reader)

core_subjects.subject: Optional Subject (e.g., Computer Science / Physical Education / Economics)
topics: Computer Science: (Computer Systems, Python/C++/Java Programming, Data Structures - Lists, Stacks, Queues, DBMS, SQL, Computer Networks, Cyber Ethics, Cyber Safety)
Informatics Practices: (Python, Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, Data Visualization, DBMS, SQL, Computer Networks, Societal Impacts)
Physical Education: (Sports Physiology, Biomechanics, Sports Psychology, Training Methods, Health and Nutrition, Yoga)
Economics: (Microeconomics, Macroeconomics, Statistics for Economics, Indian Economic Development)
Psychology: (Introduction to Psychology, Methods, Human Development, Sensory Processes, Learning, Memory, Motivation)

specializations: Officer in Navy (NDA, 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry)
Officer in Air Force (NDA - Flying/Ground Duty)

higher_studies.path: Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) / Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.)

degrees: B.Sc. in Nautical Science (for Merchant Navy)
National Defence Academy (NDA) (Army, Navy, Air Force)
Indian Naval Academy (INA) - 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry
B.Tech + LLB (Honours) (Specialized in Patent or Cyber Law)
B.Sc. + LLB (Honours) (Specialized in Forensic or Environmental Law)
Commercial Pilot License (CPL) from a flying school

streams.description: Focuses on medical sciences, life sciences, and paramedical fields. Ideal for students with strong empathy, aspiring to careers in healthcare, and with a keen interest in biological research.

core_subjects.subject: Physics
topics: Physical World and Measurement
Mechanics (Kinematics, Laws of Motion, Work, Energy, Power, System of Particles, Rotational Motion, Gravitation)
Properties of Bulk Matter (Elasticity, Fluid Mechanics, Surface Tension, Viscosity, Thermal Properties of Matter)
Thermodynamics (Heat, Temperature, Laws of Thermodynamics, Kinetic Theory of Gases)
Oscillations and Waves (Simple Harmonic Motion, Doppler Effect, Wave Optics)
Electrostatics (Electric Charges, Fields, Potential, Capacitance)
Current Electricity (Ohm's Law, Circuits, Kirchhoff's Laws, Electrical Instruments)
Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism (Magnetic Fields, Forces, Torque)
Electromagnetic Induction and AC (Faraday's Law, Lenz's Law, AC Circuits)
Electromagnetic Waves
Optics (Ray Optics, Reflection, Refraction, Lenses, Prisms, Microscopes, Telescopes)
Modern Physics (Dual Nature of Matter, Photoelectric Effect, Atoms, Nuclei, Radioactivity)
Electronic Devices (Semiconductors, Diodes, Transistors)

core_subjects.subject: Chemistry
topics: Basic Concepts (Mole Concept, Stoichiometry, Atomic Structure, Periodicity)
Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure (VSEPR, Hybridization, VBT, MOT)
States of Matter (Gases, Liquids, Solids)
Thermodynamics and Equilibrium (Enthalpy, Entropy, Gibbs Free Energy, Chemical Equilibrium, Ionic Equilibrium)
Redox Reactions and Electrochemistry (Electrochemical Cells, Nernst Equation, Electrolysis)
Chemical Kinetics (Rate of Reaction, Order of Reaction, Arrhenius Equation)
Solutions (Colligative Properties, Vapour Pressure)
Surface Chemistry (Adsorption, Colloids, Emulsions)
Metallurgy (Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements)
s-Block, p-Block, d-Block, and f-Block Elements
Coordination Compounds (Nomenclature, Isomerism, VBT, CFT)
Organic Chemistry - Basic Principles (GOC, Nomenclature, Isomerism, Reaction Mechanisms)
Hydrocarbons (Alkanes, Alkenes, Alkynes, Arenes)
Organic Compounds Containing Halogens, Oxygen, Nitrogen (Alcohols, Phenols, Ethers, Aldehydes, Ketones, Carboxylic Acids, Amines, Diazonium Salts)
Biomolecules (Carbohydrates, Proteins, Vitamins, Nucleic Acids, Enzymes, Hormones)
Polymers (Classification, Polymerization, Common Polymers)
Chemistry in Everyday Life (Drugs, Medicines, Soaps, Detergents, Food Preservatives)

core_subjects.subject: Biology
topics: The Living World (Diversity, Taxonomy, Classification)
Biological Classification (Kingdoms - Monera, Protista, Fungi, Plantae, Animalia, Viruses, Viroids, Lichens)
Plant Kingdom (Algae, Bryophytes, Pteridophytes, Gymnosperms, Angiosperms)
Animal Kingdom (Classification, Phyla from Porifera to Chordata)
Morphology and Anatomy of Flowering Plants (Root, Stem, Leaf, Flower, Fruit, Tissues)
Structural Organisation in Animals (Tissues, Cockroach/Earthworm/Frog Anatomy)
Cell: The Unit of Life (Cell Theory, Prokaryotic vs Eukaryotic, Cell Organelles, Cell Membrane, Cell Wall)
Biomolecules (Primary and Secondary Metabolites, Proteins, Polysaccharides, Nucleic Acids, Lipids, Enzymes)
Cell Cycle and Cell Division (Mitosis, Meiosis)
Plant Physiology (Transport in Plants, Mineral Nutrition, Photosynthesis, Respiration, Plant Growth and Development)

Human Physiology (Digestion and Absorption, Breathing and Respiration, Body Fluids and Circulation, Excretory Products and Elimination, Locomotion and Movement, Neural Control and Coordination, Chemical Coordination and Integration - Hormones)
Reproduction (Reproduction in Organisms, Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Human Reproduction, Reproductive Health)

Genetics and Evolution (Mendelian Inheritance, Chromosomal Theory, Sex Determination, Linkage and Recombination, Molecular Basis of Inheritance - DNA, RNA, Genetic Code, Gene Expression, Human Genome Project, DNA Fingerprinting, Evolution - Theories and Evidence)
Biology and Human Welfare (Health and Disease, Immunity, AIDS, Cancer, Drugs, Microbes in Human Welfare - Household, Industrial, Sewage Treatment, Biogas)
Biotechnology (Principles and Processes, Recombinant DNA Technology, Applications in Agriculture, Medicine, Transgenic Animals)
Ecology and Environment (Organisms and Populations, Ecosystems, Biodiversity and Conservation, Environmental Issues - Pollution, Climate Change)

core_subjects.subject: English
topics: Reading Comprehension (Unseen Passages, Note Making, Summarizing)
Writing Skills (Notice, Letter Writing - Formal/Informal, Report Writing, Article, Speech, Debate, Resume, Job Application)
Grammar (Tenses, Modals, Voice, Narration, Clauses, Transformation of Sentences)
Literature (Prose, Poetry, Supplementary Reader)

core_subjects.subject: Optional Subject (e.g., Psychology / Biotechnology / Home Science / Mathematics)
topics: Psychology: (What is Psychology?, Methods of Enquiry, Human Development, Sensory Processes, Learning, Human Memory, Motivation and Emotion, Psychological Disorders)
Biotechnology: (Recombinant DNA Technology, Genetic Engineering, Industrial Biotechnology, Bioinformatics Basics)
Home Science: (Human Development, Nutrition, Resource Management, Textiles and Apparel, Communication and Extension)
Mathematics: (See PCM topics, often chosen for B.Tech in Biotechnology or Bioinformatics)
Physical Education: (Sports Physiology, Biomechanics, Sports Psychology, Training Methods, Health and Nutrition)

specializations: MD (Doctor of Medicine - Specialist, e.g., Cardiologist, Dermatologist, Neurologist, Pediatrician)
MS (Master of Surgery - Specialist, e.g., Orthopaedic Surgeon, Neurosurgeon, ENT Surgeon)
MDS (Master of Dental Surgery - e.g., Orthodontist, Endodontist, Prosthodontist, Oral Surgeon)

degrees: MBBS (Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery)
BAMS (Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery)
BHMS (Bachelor of Homeopathic Medicine and Surgery)
BUMS (Bachelor of Unani Medicine and Surgery)
BNYS (Bachelor of Naturopathy and Yogic Sciences)
BASLP (Bachelor of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology)
B.V.Sc & AH (Bachelor of Veterinary Science and Animal Husbandry)

higher_studies.path: Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) in Life Sciences
Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) in Applied Sciences

degrees: B.Tech in Biotechnology (if Maths was optional or via specific exams)
BBA in Hospital Administration / Healthcare Management

streams.description: Focuses on business, finance, accounting, and economics. Ideal for students interested in corporate careers, financial markets, entrepreneurship, and management.

core_subjects.subject: Accountancy
topics: Introduction to Accounting (Objectives, Terminology, Principles, Concepts, GAAP)
Theory Base of Accounting (Accounting Standards - Ind AS/IFRS basics)
Recording of Transactions (Journal, Ledger, Cash Book, Subsidiary Books)
Trial Balance and Rectification of Errors
Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS)
Depreciation, Provisions, and Reserves
Bills of Exchange (Promissory Notes, Accommodation Bills)
Financial Statements of Sole Proprietorship (Trading Account, Profit & Loss, Balance Sheet)
Accounting for Not-for-Profit Organizations (NPO)
Partnership Firms (Fundamentals, Admission, Retirement, Death, Dissolution, Goodwill)
Company Accounts (Issue of Shares, Forfeiture, Re-issue, Issue of Debentures)
Analysis of Financial Statements (Comparative Statements, Common-Size Statements, Ratio Analysis - Liquidity, Solvency, Activity, Profitability)
Cash Flow Statement (Operating, Investing, Financing Activities)
Computerised Accounting (Introduction to Tally/ERP systems, Database concepts)

core_subjects.subject: Business Studies
topics: Foundations of Business (Nature and Purpose of Business, History of Trade)
Forms of Business Organisations (Sole Proprietorship, Partnership, Company, Cooperative, HUF)
Public, Private, and Global Enterprises (MNCs, Public Sector, Joint Ventures)
Business Services (Banking, Insurance, Warehousing, Postal, Telecom)
Emerging Modes of Business (E-Business, BPO, KPO, Outsourcing)
Social Responsibility and Business Ethics (CSR, Environmental Protection)
Sources of Business Finance (Owned Funds, Borrowed Funds, Equity, Debt, Retained Earnings)
Small Business and Entrepreneurship (Start-up India, MSME, Intellectual Property Rights - IPR)
Internal Trade (Wholesale, Retail, GST)
International Business (Export-Import Procedures, WTO, Foreign Trade)
Nature and Significance of Management (Principles, Levels, Functions)
Principles of Management (Fayol's 14 Principles, Taylor's Scientific Management)
Business Environment (Dimensions: Economic, Social, Legal, Political, Technological)
Planning (Process, Types of Plans)
Organising (Structure, Delegation, Decentralization)
Staffing (Recruitment, Selection, Training, Development)
Directing (Motivation - Maslow's Hierarchy, Leadership, Communication)
Controlling (Process, Techniques)
Financial Management (Investment, Financing, Dividend Decisions, Capital Structure, Working Capital)
Financial Markets (Money Market - Instruments, Capital Market - Primary/Secondary, SEBI)
Marketing Management (Functions, Marketing Mix - 4Ps: Product, Price, Place, Promotion, Branding, Labelling)
Consumer Protection (Consumer Rights, Responsibilities, CPA 2019, Grievance Redressal)

core_subjects.subject: Economics
topics: Microeconomics (Introduction, Central Problems, Consumer's Equilibrium - Utility/Indifference Curve, Demand, Elasticity of Demand, Production Function, Cost, Revenue, Supply, Forms of Market - Perfect Competition, Monopoly, Monopolistic, Oligopoly, Price Determination)

Statistics for Economics (Collection, Organisation, Presentation of Data - Tabular/Graphical, Measures of Central Tendency - Mean, Median, Mode, Measures of Dispersion - Standard Deviation, Variance, Correlation, Index Numbers - WPI, CPI)

Macroeconomics (National Income and Related Aggregates - GDP, GNP, NNP, Money and Banking - Central Bank/Commercial Bank functions, Monetary Policy, Determination of Income and Employment - AD/AS model, Keynesian Theory, Government Budget - Objectives, Components, Fiscal Policy, Fiscal Deficit, Balance of Payments - BoP, Foreign Exchange Rate)

Indian Economic Development (Indian Economy on the eve of Independence, Five Year Plans 1950-1990, Economic Reforms since 1991 - LPG, Poverty, Human Capital Formation, Rural Development, Employment, Infrastructure, Sustainable Development, India-China-Pakistan Comparative Development)

core_subjects.subject: English
topics: Reading Comprehension (Unseen Passages, Note Making, Summarizing)
Writing Skills (Notice, Letter Writing - Formal/Informal, Report Writing, Article, Speech, Debate, Resume, Job Application)
Grammar (Tenses, Modals, Voice, Narration, Clauses, Transformation of Sentences)
Literature (Prose, Poetry, Supplementary Reader)

core_subjects.subject: Optional Subject (e.g., Mathematics / Informatics Practices / Legal Studies)
topics: Mathematics (Core): (See PCM topics, essential for B.Sc. Economics, B.Sc. Statistics, Actuarial Science)
Applied Mathematics: (Financial Mathematics, Compound Interest, Annuities, Linear Programming, Matrices, Calculus, Statistics, Probability Distributions)
Informatics Practices (IP): (Python Programming, Pandas DataFrames, Data Visualization with Matplotlib/Pyplot, Database Management, SQL Queries - Joins, Group By, Computer Networks, Cyber Ethics)
Legal Studies: (Theory of Law, Types of Law, Indian Constitution, Judiciary, Civil and Criminal Law, Legal Maxims, Human Rights)
Physical Education: (Sports Physiology, Biomechanics, Sports Psychology, Training Methods, Health and Nutrition)

specializations: Certified Management Accountant (CMA / CIMA) - (Costing, Management Accounting)
Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA) - (Investment Management, Portfolio Analysis)
ACCA (Association of Chartered Certified Accountants - UK)
Bank PO (Probationary Officer) / Bank Manager (Public/Private Sector)
Development Sector Professional (NGOs, World Bank, IMF)

degrees: CFA (Chartered Financial Analyst - Level 1 during graduation)
MBA (Master of Business Administration) - (Finance, Marketing, HR, Operations, IT, International Business, Business Analytics)

streams.description: Focuses on human society, culture, language, history, and creative arts. Offers a wide range of subjects and is ideal for creative minds, empathetic individuals, and those interested in social sciences, public policy, law, or civil services.

core_subjects.subject: History
topics: Themes in World History (Early Societies, Empires - Roman/Greek, Nomadic Empires, Modernization - Industrial Revolution, World Wars)
Themes in Indian History (Harappan Civilization, Vedic Period, Mauryan, Gupta, Mughal Empire, Vijayanagara Empire, Colonialism, Nationalist Movement, Partition)
Framing the Constitution
Art and Architecture History (Ancient, Medieval, Modern)
Social History (Caste, Gender, Tribal Societies)

core_subjects.subject: Political Science
topics: Indian Constitution at Work (Rights, Executive, Legislature, Judiciary, Federalism, Local Government)
Political Theory (Liberty, Equality, Justice, Rights, Secularism, Nationalism, Peace, Development)
Contemporary World Politics (Cold War, US Hegemony, Alternative Centers of Power - EU/ASEAN, South Asia, International Organizations - UN, Globalization)
Politics in India Since Independence (Nation-Building, One-Party Dominance, Party Systems, Democratic Resurgence, Indian Foreign Policy, Regional Aspirations)

core_subjects.subject: Sociology
topics: Introducing Sociology (Society, Social Institutions - Family/Marriage/Kinship, Culture, Socialization)
Understanding Society (Social Structure, Stratification - Class/Caste/Gender, Social Change, Environment and Society)
Indian Society (Demographic Structure, Social Institutions, Cultural Diversity, Social Inequality and Exclusion)
Social Change and Development in India (Structural Change, Cultural Change, The Constitution and Social Change, Globalization, Mass Media)

core_subjects.subject: Psychology
topics: What is Psychology? (Introduction, Branches, Methods)
Methods of Enquiry in Psychology (Goals, Steps, Research Methods - Observation, Experimental, Correlational)
Human Development (Lifespan Perspective, Cognitive Development - Piaget, Moral Development - Kohlberg)
Sensory, Attentional and Perceptual Processes (Sensation, Perception, Attention)
Learning (Classical Conditioning, Operant Conditioning, Observational Learning)
Human Memory (Models - Atkinson-Shiffrin, Levels of Processing, Forgetting)
Thinking (Problem Solving, Reasoning, Decision Making, Creative Thinking)
Motivation and Emotion (Theories of Motivation - Maslow, Theories of Emotion)
Psychological Disorders (Anxiety, Somatic, Dissociative, Mood, Schizophrenic, Substance-Use)
Therapeutic Approaches (Psychodynamic, Behavioural, Cognitive, Humanistic, Biomedical, Yoga)

core_subjects.subject: Geography
topics: Fundamentals of Physical Geography (The Earth - Origin/Evolution, Interior, Landforms - Fluvial/Glacial/Arid, Climate, Water - Oceans/Circulation, Soils, Biodiversity)
Fundamentals of Human Geography (Population - Distribution/Density/Growth, Human Activities - Primary/Secondary/Tertiary/Quaternary, Transport, Communication, International Trade)
India - Physical Environment (Physiography, Drainage Systems, Climate, Natural Vegetation, Soils)
India - People and Economy (Population, Human Settlements, Land Resources, Water Resources, Mineral and Energy Resources, Transport, Trade, GIS/GPS Basics)

core_subjects.subject: Economics
topics: Statistics for Economics (Collection, Organisation, Presentation of Data, Measures of Central Tendency, Dispersion, Correlation, Index Numbers)
Microeconomics (Introduction, Consumer's Equilibrium, Demand, Supply, Forms of Market, Price Determination)
Macroeconomics (National Income, Money and Banking, Determination of Income and Employment, Government Budget, Balance of Payments)
Indian Economic Development (Indian Economy 1950-1990, Economic Reforms since 1991, Poverty, Human Capital Formation, Rural Development, Sustainable Development)

core_subjects.subject: English / Other Language (e.g., Hindi, Sanskrit, French, German)
topics: Advanced Literature (Prose, Poetry, Drama, Novels)
Creative Writing (Short Story, Poetry, Script Writing)
Advanced Grammar and Composition
Reading Comprehension (Literary and Non-Literary Texts)
Linguistics (Phonetics, Morphology - if specialized)
History of Literature

core_subjects.subject: Optional (e.g., Fine Arts / Legal Studies / Home Science / Philosophy)
topics: Fine Arts: (Painting, Sculpture, Graphic Design, History of Art)
Legal Studies: (Theory of Law, Judiciary, Legal Maxims, Human Rights, Property Law, Contract Law)
Home Science: (Food and Nutrition, Human Development, Resource Management, Textiles and Apparel)
Philosophy: (Introduction to Western and Indian Philosophy, Logic, Ethics, Epistemology)
Physical Education: (Sports Physiology, Biomechanics, Sports Psychology, Training Methods)
Music (Vocal or Instrumental): (Music Theory, History of Music, Practical Performance)

career_options.career: Civil Services and Public Administration (UPSC, State PSC)

degrees: B.A. (Honours) in a Foreign Language (French, German, Spanish, Japanese, etc.)
B.Des (Bachelor of Design) in Fashion, Interior, Graphic, UI/UX, Product, Animation
BFA (Bachelor of Fine Arts) in Painting, Sculpture, Applied Arts
BJMC (Bachelor of Journalism and Mass Communication)
B.A. in Film, Television & New Media Production
BBA in Travel and Tourism Management
Integrated M.A. in Liberal Arts / Humanities

streams.description: 3-year technical diploma programs focused on practical engineering and other fields, offering early specialization, industry skills, and a direct path to employment or lateral entry into B.Tech.

core_subjects.subject: Diploma in Computer Engineering / IT
topics: Computer Hardware and Maintenance
Programming in C/C++/Java/Python
Data Structures and Algorithms
Database Management Systems (DBMS, SQL)
Operating Systems (Windows, Linux)
Computer Networks and Security
Web Development (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, PHP/Node.js)
Software Engineering
Mobile Application Development (Basics)

core_subjects.subject: Diploma in Mechanical Engineering
topics: Engineering Drawing / CAD (AutoCAD, SolidWorks)
Applied Thermodynamics
Strength of Materials (SOM)
Manufacturing Technology / Workshop Practice
Theory of Machines (TOM)
Machine Design
Fluid Mechanics and Hydraulics
Refrigeration and Air Conditioning (RAC)
Industrial Management
CNC Machines and Automation

core_subjects.subject: Diploma in Civil Engineering
topics: Surveying (Chain, Compass, Theodolite, Total Station)
Building Materials and Construction Technology
Structural Mechanics / Theory of Structures (TOS)
Concrete Technology
Transportation Engineering (Highways, Railways)
Geotechnical Engineering (Soil Mechanics)
Water Resources Engineering (Irrigation, Hydrology)
Estimating and Costing
Civil Engineering Drawing (AutoCAD)

core_subjects.subject: Diploma in Electrical Engineering
topics: Basic Electrical Engineering
Electrical Machines (DC/AC Motors, Generators, Transformers)
Power Systems (Generation, Transmission, Distribution)
Electronics Devices and Circuits (Analog/Digital)
Electrical Measurements and Instrumentation
Power Electronics
Control Systems
PLC and SCADA (Basics)
Electrical Installation and Maintenance

core_subjects.subject: Other Diplomas
topics: Electronics and Communication (ECE): (Analog/Digital Circuits, Communication Systems, Microprocessors, VLSI)
Automobile Engineering: (Engine Systems, Chassis, Transmission, Auto Electrical, Vehicle Maintenance)
Chemical Engineering: (Fluid Flow, Heat Transfer, Mass Transfer, Chemical Technology, Process Instrumentation)
Architecture Assistantship: (Building Design, Construction, Graphics, CAD, History of Architecture)
Interior Design: (Design Principles, Materials, CAD, Furniture Design, Building Services)
Hotel Management: (Front Office, Housekeeping, Food Production, F&B Service)
Medical Laboratory Technology (DMLT): (Anatomy, Physiology, Biochemistry, Pathology, Microbiology)

specializations: Junior Engineer (JE) in Govt. Depts (PWD, CPWD, Irrigation, PHE)
JE in Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs) (BHEL, GAIL, NTPC, ONGC)
Technical Assistant in Defence (DRDO, Ordnance Factories)
Software Support Professional / Help Desk Analyst

higher_studies.path: Lateral Entry to B.Tech / B.E. / B.Arch
degrees: Direct admission into the 2nd year (3rd semester) of a B.Tech/B.E. program in the same specialization (e.g., Diploma in Mech. -> B.Tech in Mech.)
Direct admission into the 2nd year of B.Arch (after Diploma in Architecture Assistantship)
AMIE (Associate Member of the Institution of Engineers) - (Sections A & B, recognized as equivalent to B.E./B.Tech)
B.Sc. in Engineering (less common, offered by some institutes)
B.Sc. in IT / Computer Science (sometimes allows lateral entry)
B.A. or B.Com (if changing fields, starts from 1st year)
Post-Diploma in specialized fields (e.g., Tool Design, Industrial Safety, PLC/SCADA)
Professional IT Certifications (e.g., CCNA, Red Hat, AWS Cloud Practitioner)

streams.description: 1-2 year trade courses focused on specific hands-on technical skills for quick employment in industrial, technical, and service sectors. Ideal for students who prefer practical, skill-based learning.

core_subjects.subject: Electrician
topics: Trade Safety and Precautions
Hand and Power Tools
Basic Electrical Principles (Ohm's Law, Kirchhoff's Laws)
Electrical Wiring (Residential, Commercial, Industrial)
Motor Winding (DC/AC)
Transformer Maintenance and Repair
Basic Electronics (Diodes, Transistors, Rectifiers)
Circuit Installation and Repair
Earthing and Grounding
Installation and Maintenance of Electrical Appliances

core_subjects.subject: Fitter
topics: Trade Safety
Hand Tools and Operations (Filing, Sawing, Drilling, Tapping)
Precision Instruments (Vernier Caliper, Micrometer)
Lathe Operations (Turning, Facing, Tapering)
Milling, Grinding, and Drilling Operations
Welding (Arc, Gas)
Sheet Metal Work
Pipe Fitting and Plumbing Basics
Assembly and Disassembly of Machine Parts
Bearings, Gears, and Pulleys

core_subjects.subject: Mechanic (Motor Vehicle - MMV)
topics: Automotive Safety and Tools
Engine Systems (Petrol, Diesel) - Overhauling
Transmission Systems (Clutch, Gearbox, Propeller Shaft)
Braking Systems (Drum, Disc, Hydraulic, ABS)
Suspension and Steering Systems
Auto-Electrical Systems (Battery, Alternator, Starter Motor, Wiring)
Vehicle Diagnostics and Repair
Wheel Alignment and Balancing
Basic Auto-AC
Pollution Control (BS-VI norms)

core_subjects.subject: COPA (Computer Operator and Programming Assistant)
topics: Computer Fundamentals and Hardware
Operating Systems (Windows, Linux)
MS Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Access)
Database Management Systems (DBMS, SQL Basics)
Programming with JavaScript
Visual Basic for Applications (VBA)
Basic Web Page Design (HTML, CSS)
Cyber Security and Internet Basics
E-commerce Concepts
Soft Skills and Communication

core_subjects.subject: Draughtsman (Civil / Mechanical)
topics: Engineering Drawing (Projections, Sections, Views)
CAD Software (AutoCAD 2D and 3D)
Building Plans, Elevations, and Sections (Civil)
Surveying and Levelling Basics (Civil)
Reinforced Concrete (RCC) Drawing (Civil)
Machine Part Drawing and Assembly (Mechanical)
Limits, Fits, and Tolerances (Mechanical)
Revit or other 3D modeling software (Basics)

core_subjects.subject: Other Trades (Examples)
topics: Refrigeration and AC Mechanic (RAC): (Refrigeration Cycle, Compressors, Condensers, Installation, Repair, Gas Charging)
Welder: (Arc Welding, Gas Welding, TIG, MIG, Plasma Cutting, Reading Blueprints)
Plumber: (Pipe Fitting, Sanitation Systems, Water Supply Systems, Fixture Installation, Repair)
Turner: (Advanced Lathe Operations, Thread Cutting, CNC Lathe Programming Basics)
Dress Making / Fashion Design: (Pattern Making, Cutting, Sewing, Garment Construction, Fashion Illustration)
Digital Photographer: (Camera Operations, Lighting, Composition, Photo Editing - Photoshop/Lightroom)
Hospital Housekeeping: (Sanitation, Biomedical Waste Management, Patient Room Maintenance, Infection Control)

specializations: Plant Operator (e.g., in Power Plants, Chemical Plants)
Technician in Railways (ALP - Assistant Loco Pilot, Technician)
Technician in PSUs (BHEL, GAIL, NTPC, etc.)

degrees: Lateral entry (direct admission) into the 2nd year of a Diploma in Engineering (e.g., ITI Electrician -> Diploma in Electrical Engineering)
National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) from a company (e.g., Railways, PSUs, Private Industry). This combines on-the-job training with a stipend.
CITS (Craft Instructor Training Scheme) - To become an instructor in an ITI
Complete 12th grade (NIOS) to become eligible for non-technical degree courses (B.A., B.Com, etc.)

streams.description: 1-3 year diploma or certificate courses in allied health sciences, preparing students for skilled, hands-on support roles in the healthcare industry. Ideal for those wanting a quick entry into a medical-field job.

core_subjects.subject: Diploma in Medical Laboratory Technology (DMLT)
topics: Human Anatomy and Physiology
Clinical Biochemistry (Blood Glucose, Renal/Liver Function Tests)
Clinical Pathology (Urine, Stool, Semen Analysis)
Microbiology (Bacterial Staining, Culture, Sensitivity)
Hematology (Blood Cell Counts, Hemoglobin, Blood Groups)
Blood Banking (Cross-matching, Transfusion)
Histopathology (Tissue Processing, Staining)
Lab Procedures, Safety, and Waste Management

core_subjects.subject: Diploma in Radiology / X-Ray Technology (DMRT/DRT)
topics: Human Anatomy and Physiology
Radiation Physics and Safety
Radiographic Techniques (Positioning for X-Rays)
Patient Care and Ethics
Medical Imaging Modalities (X-Ray, CT, MRI, Ultrasound basics)
Darkroom Procedures / Digital Image Processing
Radiotherapy Basics (in some courses)

core_subjects.subject: Diploma in Operation Theatre Technology (DOTT)
topics: Surgical Instruments (Identification, Handling, Sterilization)
Anaesthesia Technology (Drugs, Machines, Patient Monitoring)
Sterilization Procedures (Autoclaving, Fumigation)
Patient Monitoring (Vital Signs, ECG)
Surgical Assistant Skills (Suturing, Draping)
Infection Control in OT
Basic Life Support (BLS) and CPR
Post-Operative Care

core_subjects.subject: Other Diplomas / Certificates
topics: Diploma in Dialysis Technology: (Kidney Anatomy/Physiology, Principles of Dialysis, Hemodialysis Machine, Patient Care, Water Treatment)
Diploma in Optometry (D.Optom): (Eye Anatomy, Refraction, Contact Lenses, Optical Instruments, Common Eye Diseases)
Diploma in Dental Hygiene: (Oral Anatomy, Dental Materials, Scaling and Polishing, Dental X-Rays, Community Dentistry)
Diploma in Medical Records Technology (DMRT): (Medical Terminology, Record Keeping, ICD-10 Coding, Hospital Statistics, Medico-Legal Aspects)
Diploma in Emergency Medical Services (Paramedic): (Trauma Care, BLS, Advanced Cardiac Life Support - ACLS, Triage, Ambulance Operations)

specializations: Dental Hygienist / Dental Assistant (in Dental Clinics)
Pharmacist Assistant (with D.Pharm - Diploma in Pharmacy)
Medical Sales Representative (for devices related to trade)

degrees: Lateral entry (direct 2nd year) to B.Sc. in the respective field (e.g., DMLT -> B.Sc. in MLT)
Lateral entry to B.Sc. in Radiology / Imaging Technology
Lateral entry to B.Sc. in Operation Theatre Technology
Lateral entry to B.Sc. in Optometry
Fresh admission to B.Sc. Nursing (if eligible with 12th Science)
Advanced or specialized Post-Graduate Diploma courses in the same field (e.g., Advanced Diploma in Cardiac Care)
Lateral entry into B.Voc programs related to healthcare (e.g., B.Voc in MLT, B.Voc in Patient Care Management)
Advanced certifications in specific areas (e.g., Medical Coding - CPC, Advanced Cardiac Life Support - ACLS)
